pattern: Sliding window over the string tracks the highest frequency character in the window; a window is valid when its length minus that frequency is at most k.
hints: A contiguous window can be made uniform by changing all non-dominant characters; the cost equals window length minus the count of the most frequent character.
Expand the right end of the window greedily and contract the left end whenever the replacement cost exceeds k.
Maintain a frequency map of characters in the window and track the running maximum frequency; the window shrinks only when length - maxFreq > k.

edge_inputs.description: alternating two characters, k large enough to unify everything
string with one replacement creating the best window
k is zero so no replacements allowed; answer is longest run
k equals string length so entire string is the answer
all characters already the same, no replacement needed
k large relative to length but two distinct clusters compete
best window is in the middle

stress.description: long random uppercase string with varied k values
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    s = ''.join(rng.choice('ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZ') for _ in range(n))
    k = rng.randint(0, n)
    return [s, k]
